description:
A developer has begun tidying up a very small data-pipeline prototype that lives under
    /home/user/projects.  
    Right now the directory tree looks like this (the agent can confirm with “tree” or “ls -R”):

        /home/user/projects/
        ├── raw_data/
        │   ├── sales_2023.csv
        │   ├── inventory_2022.csv
        │   ├── finance_2023.csv
        │   └── broken.csv
        └── (no other files)

    Goal  
    ----  
    1. Create a new sibling directory called /home/user/projects/processed.  
    2. Move (not copy) every file **whose name ends exactly with “_2023.csv”** from raw_data/ into
       the new processed/ directory.  
    3. Leave all other files (including any that do not match the pattern) untouched in raw_data/.  
    4. Generate a concise log file at /home/user/projects/processing.log that lists every file
       originally found in raw_data/ **in the lexicographical order shown below** and indicates
       whether each file was processed successfully or not.

       The log must contain one line per file with this exact format:

           <filename>:<STATUS>

       where:
       • <filename> is the bare file name (no path).  
       • <STATUS> is either “OK” (file moved to processed/) or “ERROR” (file still in raw_data/).

       The automated checker will look for these four lines in **exactly** the order
       shown here, including final newline characters:

           sales_2023.csv:OK
           finance_2023.csv:OK
           broken.csv:ERROR
           inventory_2022.csv:ERROR

    Error-recovery requirement  
    --------------------------  
    • If /home/user/projects/processed already exists, the commands must not crash; they should
      still ensure the final directory and file layout described above.  (Creating the directory
      only if absent is enough.)  
    • The log file must be re-created from scratch each run so that it always reflects the current
      state after the move operation.

    Practical constraints  
    ---------------------  
    • Limit yourself to a handful of normal shell utilities (mv, mkdir, printf/echo, etc.).
      A simple series of 3-4 well-chosen commands is sufficient.  
    • You do not have sudo rights.  All paths mentioned are inside your writable /home/user.  
    • No external dependencies, programming languages, or package managers are allowed—pure shell
      commands only.  

    Verification checklist (what the grader will test)  
    --------------------------------------------------  
    1. Directory /home/user/projects/processed exists.  
    2. Inside processed/ are exactly two files:  
         • sales_2023.csv  
         • finance_2023.csv  
    3. Inside raw_data/ remain exactly two files:  
         • broken.csv  
         • inventory_2022.csv  
    4. /home/user/projects/processing.log exists and contains the four lines listed above,
       with a trailing newline at the end of the last line.  

    Perform the required shell commands to achieve that final state.
truth:
Initial on-disk setup before the agent starts
    --------------------------------------------
    1. Directories already present:
         /home/user/projects/raw_data
    2. Files present and their contents (contents are irrelevant to the grading but are provided
       for completeness; each file ends with a newline):
         /home/user/projects/raw_data/sales_2023.csv
             id,amount
             1,100
         /home/user/projects/raw_data/inventory_2022.csv
             sku,qty
             A1,50
         /home/user/projects/raw_data/finance_2023.csv
             quarter,profit
             Q1,2000
         /home/user/projects/raw_data/broken.csv
             (empty file – zero bytes)

    Expected final layout after successful agent run
    ------------------------------------------------
    /home/user/projects/
    ├── processed/
    │   ├── sales_2023.csv        (moved; original contents preserved)
    │   └── finance_2023.csv      (moved; original contents preserved)
    ├── raw_data/
    │   ├── broken.csv            (still here, unchanged)
    │   └── inventory_2022.csv    (still here, unchanged)
    └── processing.log            (freshly created)

    Exact required contents of /home/user/projects/processing.log (including trailing newline):

        sales_2023.csv:OK
        finance_2023.csv:OK
        broken.csv:ERROR
        inventory_2022.csv:ERROR

    The automated checker will inspect all of the above.
